title: Was Dr Katsuragi a pawn for SEELE
display_title: Was Dr Katsuragi a pawn for SEELE
wikitext:
[]: Was Dr. Katsuragi a pawn for SEELE?             Quote Post by Replicator » Mon Dec 17, 2012 4:20 pm Dr. Katsuragi first proposed the "Super Solenoid" theory, which we know as the S2 Engine/Fruit of Life for the Angels. But didn't he suggest that the S2 Engine could potentially be used as a new form of energy , rather than a catalyst for the Instrumentality Project? Here's the thing, it's unclear how much Dr. Katsuragi knew about the nature of Adam/Lilith, and at the time, the experiment in Antarctica was only to test the S2 theory. The team excavated Adam, who was the centre of the experiment, but how would they have known Adam would be the "prototype" for the S2 Engine? My guess is SEELE probably was involved, and (covertly) backed Dr. Katsuragi's research, and provided some clues about Adam (from the Dead Sea Scrolls) and its power source. Dr. Katsuragi was probably purely concerned about the scientific aspects of the S2 Engine, rather than SEELE's interest in Adam as a method of performing their version of Instrumentality. This would have effectively meant that Dr. Katsuragi and SEELE were on two different pages all together about the Second Impact. Unbeknownst to the Katsuragi Expedition, the experiment with Adam would have far deadlier consequences than they assumed, but was all according to SEELE's plans (i.e. Gendo escaped Antarctica the day before 2I). Dr. Katsuragi's work would only be to serve as a vehicle for SEELE's interests, and was in a sense a "necessary loss" for them. Discuss...

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Dec 17, 2012 4:47 pm Well, they certainly didn't tell him that his experiment would blow up on him.

[]: Re: Was Dr. Katsuragi a pawn for SEELE?             Quote Post by pioneer » Mon Dec 17, 2012 7:12 pm Replicator wrote: Dr. Katsuragi was probably purely concerned about the scientific aspects of the S2 Engine, rather than SEELE's interest in Adam as a method of performing their version of Instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Mon Dec 17, 2012 8:28 pm It's the fight between science and religion!

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Dec 17, 2012 8:44 pm Well, there's Keel's Dialogue from episode 21 (and Gendo sucking up to him - episode 12 suggests that his actual opinions on science are at least a bit different), classic religious fanatic complaining about scientists. Even if he probably has something of a point - One of the few things we know about Dr. K is how he was very absorbed in his work. He may have been to busy staring into his miscroscope to notice how all the suspicious people were leaving. Imagine the following Ironyfest: Gendo: My wife is pregnant, gimme some parenting advice! Since we won't be meeting again, I might as well ask you. Dr K: Uh... I'm sure there's a "for Dummies" guide for that?"

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Mon Dec 17, 2012 8:52 pm On the one side we have a workaholic father who ends up meddling with forces he doesn't understand, but wants a brighter future for all mankind. On the other a bunch of manipulitive old men willing to commit untold amounts of murder so as to validate their beliefs.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Tue Dec 18, 2012 3:38 am Darkwing wrote: On the one side we have a workaholic father who ends up meddling with forces he doesn't understand, but wants a brighter future for all mankind. On the other a bunch of manipulitive old men willing to commit untold amounts of murder so as to validate their beliefs.
